description:
You are working as an on-call container specialist who was just paged about a broken deployment.  
Two versioned service directories already exist:

1. /home/user/microservices/auth-service-1.2.3  
2. /home/user/microservices/billing-service-2.4.0  

Your immediate job is to expose the **currently-active** build of each micro-service to other automation tools by creating two symbolic links inside the same parent directory, then produce a tiny status report file.

Required end state:

A. Symbolic links  
   • /home/user/microservices/current-auth must point to /home/user/microservices/auth-service-1.2.3  
   • /home/user/microservices/current-billing must point to /home/user/microservices/billing-service-2.4.0  

B. Status report  
   • Path: /home/user/microservices/symlink_report.log  
   • File owner: the regular user running the task (no root needed).  
   • Content: exactly two lines, **each terminated by a single UNIX newline (LF)** and nothing else.  
     Line-by-line format that the grader will check byte-for-byte:  

       current-auth -> auth-service-1.2.3\n  
       current-billing -> billing-service-2.4.0\n  

     (That is: the symbolic link’s basename, exactly one space, “->”, exactly one space, and the **target directory’s basename**, followed by a newline.)

What you need to do (high-level):

1. Ensure you are inside /home/user/microservices (create it if the path already isn’t present in the container).  
2. Create or replace the two symbolic links so they match section A.  
3. Generate the status report so it matches section B. It must be overwritten if it already exists.

Only 2-3 shell commands should be necessary if done efficiently. When you finish, the grader will:

• Verify that both symlinks exist and resolve to the correct absolute targets.  
• Inspect /home/user/microservices/symlink_report.log to make sure its contents match the exact two-line format shown above (including final newline).  
• Confirm ordinary user ownership and writable permissions (default umask is fine).

No other files or output are required.  
Hint: using relative paths for the link target portion is **not** acceptable; use the absolute paths specified.
truth:
Initial filesystem setup done by the grader *before* the agent starts:

Directory /home/user/microservices already exists.
Sub-directories (real, not symbolic links):  
  /home/user/microservices/auth-service-1.2.3  
  /home/user/microservices/billing-service-2.4.0  

Nothing else is in /home/user/microservices.

Expected final state:

Symbolic links:
  /home/user/microservices/current-auth  -> /home/user/microservices/auth-service-1.2.3
  /home/user/microservices/current-billing -> /home/user/microservices/billing-service-2.4.0

Status file:
/home/user/microservices/symlink_report.log
Exact byte content (including final newline):

current-auth -> auth-service-1.2.3\n
current-billing -> billing-service-2.4.0\n

The agent will succeed if both symlinks exist, resolve correctly, and the log file content matches exactly (two lines, correct spacing, trailing newline on each line).
